rationale: Both papers centrally model the same context effects (compromise, attraction, and similarity) in brand/choice modeling, and the cited 2010 study explicitly operationalizes these effects in an empirical mixed logit setting. The citing paper’s stated gap is an empirical multi-effect context model, so this citation is plausibly a direct prior method/theory reference rather than an incidental mention; with only DOI-level match evidence, it should be retained for full relation judgment.
